current.summary: Implementing struct field and integer const extraction in Go extractor; discovered import-stripping issue mid-implementation

activeWork.description: Go extractor enhancements for capmon capability monitor pipeline - adding struct field value extraction and integer const handling
activeWork.progress: Identified root cause: adding import without immediate usage in same Edit call allows goimports/gopls to strip the unused import before next edit applies. Solution: add reflect import and usage in single Edit call to prevent stripping.

blockers.issue: Go import-stripping gotcha: sequential edits cause unused imports to be stripped by goimports/gopls before next edit applies
blockers.context: Adding import in Edit 1, then code using it in Edit 2 fails because the import is removed between edits
blockers.solution: Combine import addition and usage code in a single Edit call

recentDecisions: Struct field extraction requires reflect package for runtime type inspection
Integer const extraction added alongside struct field work
Single Edit call pattern required to prevent goimports stripping unused imports
nextSteps: Revise struct field extraction implementation to add reflect import and usage code in single Edit call
Test extraction against real provider data
Verify capmon pipeline runs end-to-end with struct field + const values populated

context.notes: Critical Go editing pattern: when adding a new import AND code that uses it, always add both in a single Edit call. Sequential edits (import in one, usage in next) fail because linters strip the 'unused' import between edits. See CLAUDE.md Go Edit Patterns section.

previousSessions.summary: Fixed compile errors in pipeline.go and stress-tested capability monitor pipeline end-to-end against real provider data; questions raised about Python format capture and hook_event_category schema requirements
